active_context.conversation_reference: Item list page filtering improvements, export/batch edit debugging, and navigation reordering completed

primary_objective.content: Stage 1 Batch Editing - Item batch editor complete, Document batch editor next (final in Stage 1)
primary_objective.stage_1_status: Languoid and Collaborator batch editors complete (production). Item batch editor complete (production) - NEW STATE-OF-THE-ART with 61 fields, complex through-models, virtual fields, invalid data preservation. Item list page filtering and exports now fully functional. Document batch editor is NEXT and FINAL for Stage 1.
recent_achievements: Item batch editor fully implemented and production-ready (61 fields, 4,402 rows)
Redis caching with async rebuild for Item list (15-20s one-time, <1s cached)
Async export for Items with UUID backend + timestamp frontend filename pattern
Invalid data preservation pattern - preserves with id: null, visualizes as red, allows user correction
Spreadsheet import for Items with comprehensive parsing (collaborators, titles, languages, choices)
Client-side filtering for exports using full cached dataset (not paginated)
Export filename standardization across all models: UUID backend, YYYY-MM-DD_HHMMSS frontend
Comprehensive lessons documented in item-batch-editor-lessons.json for Document implementation
Item list page filter persistence across navigation (matches Collaborator/Languoid pattern)
Fixed batch edit filtered and export filtered to correctly apply filters (697 items bug resolved)
Added titles field to InternalItemBatchSerializer for complete client-side filtering
Item list page UI updated to match Collaborator pattern (title + count below filters)
Navigation menu reordered: Collections now before Items
primary_objective.current_phase: Stage 1 - Batch Editing (3 of 4 complete, Document next)
next_priorities: Document batch editor implementation (final batch editor in Stage 1)
Leverage item-batch-editor-lessons.json for efficient Document implementation
Stage 1 completion and handoff to Stage 2

stage_priorities.content: Five-stage development approach with Stage 0 (React migration) as foundation

stage_definitions.stage_0: React frontend architecture migration (foundation for all future UI development)
stage_definitions.stage_1: Batch editing with smart spreadsheet interface (immediate staff value)
stage_definitions.stage_2: Repository management system (Document→File transition, true ingestion)
stage_definitions.stage_3: Public/private infrastructure (virus scanning, cross-server sync, database sync)

stage_priorities.prioritization_logic: Stage 0 enables modern UI development. Stage 1 provides immediate value to daily museum staff workflows. Stage 2 improves core functionality. Stage 3 enables public access capabilities.
stage_priorities.detailed_planning: Stages 0, 1, 2, 3, and 4 have comprehensive planning documents with technical specifications
next_planning_steps: All stages (0-5) have comprehensive planning documents

context_system_completion.content: Context memory system complete with all 20 files populated and cross-referenced

protocol_established.content: Context memory protocol established, file structure designed, initial population approach agreed

comprehensive_inventory.content: Complete analysis of existing Django functionality to guide Stage 0 React recreation
scope_discovered: 4 core models (Items, Collections, Collaborators, Languoids) with full CRUD
Advanced search/filtering with 15+ filter fields for Items
comprehensive_inventory.next_phase: Begin Phase 4: Core CRUD Components recreation

separated_frontend_backend.content: Successfully switched from integrated Django static files to separated frontend/backend architecture
architecture_benefits: Hot reload for instant React development feedback
Better development experience for Stage 0 frontend work
technical_implementation: React API calls updated to http://localhost:8000/internal/v1/

development_workflow.backend_server: Terminal 1: cd app && pipenv run python manage.py runserver 8000
development_workflow.frontend_server: Terminal 2: cd frontend && npm start (opens localhost:3000)
development_workflow.api_communication: React → Django via /internal/v1/ endpoints with CSRF tokens
development_workflow.accessibility_testing: axe-core runs automatically in development mode
mandatory_requirements: Responsive design testing on multiple devices

phase_4_item_crud_foundation.content: Phase 4 Item CRUD foundation established - core functionality implemented with related model editing explicitly deferred

honest_assessment.scope: Core Item model CRUD with foundational patterns established
honest_assessment.reality_check: Related model editing explicitly deferred per user decision
honest_assessment.user_explicit_statement: We'll circle back once we have basic CRUD for all models needed

what_was_actually_completed: ✅ List view with clickable navigation and responsive design
✅ Detail view with complete metadata display and card layout
✅ In-place editing for direct Item fields (text, choice, date, boolean, validation)
✅ ItemTitle relationship editing (titles with language selection)
✅ Secure delete with double confirmation and group-based permissions
explicitly_deferred_work: ❌ Collection field editing (deferred until Collection CRUD built)
❌ Collaborator field editing (deferred until Collaborator CRUD built)
❌ Language field editing outside ItemTitle (deferred until Languoid CRUD built)

phase_4_remaining_models.content: Ready to replicate Item CRUD patterns to remaining core models
foundation_advantages: Proven architecture patterns from Item implementation
Established development workflow and testing methodology
Backend APIs already implemented for all models
remaining_models_prioritized: Languoids - Fourth priority (referenced by Items and ItemTitles)
phase_4_remaining_models.implementation_strategy: Apply established Item patterns with model-specific adaptations
phase_4_remaining_models.estimated_effort: Significantly reduced due to proven patterns and reusable components
phase_4_remaining_models.completion_criteria: Each model achieves same CRUD completeness as Items
